news.summary: Wall Street cautiously rose at the opening on Tuesday following April's inflation data. Consumer prices in April increased by 2.3% year over year, slightly lower than the predicted 2.4%, and down from the previous month's 2.4%. Monthly prices saw a 0.2% rise, bouncing back from March's 0.1% drop but missing the expected 0.3% increase. Core inflation, excluding food and energy, remained at 2.8% annually, in line with forecasts and March. Monthly core prices increased by 0.2%, up from March's 0.1% but below the expected 0.3%. Shelter saw a 0.3% rise in April, contributing over half of the overall Consumer Price Index increase. Some categories experienced price drops such as airline fares, used cars and trucks, communication services.
news.headline: Wall Street rises as inflation cools: April's consumer prices up 2.3% YoY, core inflation at 2.8%. Nasdaq 100 hits 10-week high.

news.summary: On May 13, 2025, OTC Markets Group Inc. revealed that Consensus Mining & Seigniorage Corporation (OTCQX: CMSG), a cryptocurrency mining enterprise, met the requirements to trade on the OTCQX® Best Market. OTC Markets Group Inc. manages trading for 12,000 American and international securities.
news.headline: OTC Markets Group welcomes Consensus Mining & Seigniorage Corporation to OTCQX, trading 12,000 securities.

news.summary: Renowned economist Nouriel Roubini, dubbed "Dr. Doom" for his accurate prediction of the 2008 financial crisis, criticized President Donald Trump for backing down in the trade dispute with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Roubini stated that Trump failed to secure significant concessions from China, resulting in a clear win for Xi. He cautioned that further escalation would have devastating economic effects, including soaring U.S. inflation, supply chain disruptions, and a global recession. Roubini warned that such repercussions would have jeopardized the GOP and Trump's ambitions by the 2026 midterm elections. He suggested that minor concessions from China could still be possible. For more details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Economist Nouriel Roubini criticizes Trump for backing down in trade war with China, calls Xi Jinping the clear winner. Severe consequences avoided due to tariff rollback.

news.summary: Goldman Sachs has adjusted its outlook for the S&P 500 Index, with equity strategist David J. Kostin raising the 12-month target to 6,500, up from 6,200. The three-month target has also been increased from 5,700 to 5,900, and the six-month view is now at 6,100, compared to the previous 5,900. Kostin attributed these revisions to factors such as lower tariff rates, improved economic growth, and reduced recession risk. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE: SPY) rose 0.4% to 5,860 on Tuesday, reflecting a 21% recovery since April's lows, reaching levels last seen in early March. Key drivers of this rally include...
news.headline: Goldman Sachs raises S&P 500 target to 6,500, trims recession odds due to lower tariff rates and better growth, as index climbs to 5,860.

The new series, namely Series S, Series SF, Series A, and Series F, are awaiting regulatory approval and will offer Canadian and US dollar purchase options. news.headline: Ninepoint Partners LP introduces four new mutual fund series for Crypto and AI Leaders ETF, expanding access to strategy.

news.summary: On Thursday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a decline following a cautionary statement from Federal Reserve Chair Powell regarding potentially volatile inflation in the future. UnitedHealth Group also faced significant losses after reports emerged of a criminal investigation involving the company. The market reaction led to a notable drop in the Dow Jones, indicating investor concern.
news.headline: Dow Jones falls after Powell warns of future inflation; UnitedHealth plunges on criminal probe.

news.summary: Dynatrace, Inc. (NYSE: DT) exceeded expectations in their fourth-quarter earnings report, with a 17% year-over-year revenue growth reaching $445.17 million. This surpassed the analyst consensus of $434.78 million. The adjusted earnings per share also showed improvement at 33 cents, up from the anticipated 30 cents. Looking ahead, Dynatrace provided a robust guidance forecasting revenue between $465.00 million and $470.00 million, higher than the consensus estimate of $454.51 million. The expected adjusted earnings per share for the upcoming period are $0.37 to $0.38, surpassing the estimated 0.35 cents. For the fiscal year 2026, Dynatrace expects revenue in the range of $1.950 billion to $1.965 billion in comparison to the analyst consensus of $1.940 billion. Additionally, they anticipate an Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) between $1.975 billion and $1.990 billion. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Dynatrace (NYSE: DT) exceeds Q4 expectations, revenue hits $445.17M vs $434.78M estimate. Raises 2026 forecast to $1.950B-$1.965B.

news.summary: A recent survey from Savanta demonstrates the complex nature of American sentiments leading up to Memorial Day 2025. As the nation prepares to celebrate, findings reveal a divided populace grappling with a mix of traditional values, financial burdens, and a yearning for greater purpose. While individuals gear up for holiday festivities, a sizable portion are facing economic constraints and reconsidering their priorities. The study sheds light on the multifaceted mindset of the country, showcasing the range of perspectives and challenges being navigated as the holiday approaches.
news.headline: Americans are conflicted about Memorial Day 2025, with a Savanta survey showing financial stress and desire for deeper meaning.

news.summary: In Wilmington, North Carolina, New Leaf Builders has reached a significant achievement by completing its 100th home in the Riverlights community. news.headline: New Leaf Builders completes its 100th home in Riverlights, Wilmington marking a significant milestone in the partnership.

news.summary: Ripple's recent joint motion with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to settle their long-standing lawsuit has caused a significant spike in XRP, driving its price to $2.63. The surge correlates with a boost in futures capital amounting to $1 billion, increased wallet activity, a growing utility in decentralized finance (DeFi), and higher odds for an ETF approval. The motion requests the court to permit institutional sales and reduce Ripple's penalty from $876 million to $125 million, marking a legal victory that energized traders. This legal development has also impacted the derivatives market, with futures open interest jumping by $1 billion to reach $3.42 billion across major platforms, a 41% increase from the prior week, as reported by Glassnode. Despite minimal changes in spot volume, prominent traders are amassing leveraged long positions, showing confidence in the sustainability of this market uptrend. For those interested in trading crypto futures, Plus500 offers bonuses of up to $200 without the need for wallets, providing a platform for price speculation and free paper trading to refine trading strategies. If you're new to crypto trading, there's a chance to earn rewards of up to $400 by successfully completing short... (the rest of the news is available on Benzinga.com).
news.headline: XRP price spikes to $2.63 after SEC settlement motion, with $1 billion futures capital, rising wallet activity, and growing ETF odds.

news.summary: A popular national fitness brand, Crunch Fitness, will be joining a diverse array of daily-needs retailers at Florissant Marketplace in Florissant, Missouri. This announcement was made by First National Realty Partners (FNRP), a prominent private equity commercial real estate firm, on May 15, 2025. The addition of Crunch Fitness adds to the varied lineup of retailers at the marketplace, enhancing the options available to customers. This move reflects the growing demand for fitness services in the area and is part of FNRP's strategy to attract a wide range of tenants.
news.headline: Crunch Fitness opens at Florissant Marketplace in Missouri, part of First National Realty Partners.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, it was reported that the worldwide smart manufacturing market is expected to achieve a value of USD 790.91 billion by 2030, as per a recent study by Grand View Research, Inc. This sector is widely recognized for its rapid advancement and adoption of digital technologies.

news.summary: A company specializing in contractor data and cost management, Management Controls, Inc. (MCi), has recently expanded its global reach by launching its Track Platform in Qatar. This move signifies the company's 55th international site to adopt MCi's solutions for real-time contractor monitoring, compliance, and cost management, with Qatar being its first venture into the Middle East region. The deployment occurred at a significant gas-to-liquids (GTL) plant, one of the largest in operation.
news.headline: MCi's Track Platform launched in Qatar, 55th global site, for contractor management at GTL facility.

news.summary: The Southern Banc Company, Inc. reported net income of approximately $230,000 for the first quarter of 2025, down from $340,000 in the same period in 2024. For the nine-month period ending March 31, 2025, the Company recorded net income of around $775,000, down from $1,177,000 in 2024. Net interest margins increased by 17.92% in the first quarter of 2025. Total assets on March 31, 2025, were approximately $127.7 million, up from $113.1 million on June 30, 2024. The Company's total stockholders' equity was around $16.3 million on March 31, 2025. The Bank operates five offices in Alabama. The Company's common stock trades under the symbol "SRNN".
news.headline: The Southern Banc Company, Inc. reported Q3 net income of $230,000, or $0.30 per share, compared to $340,000 in 2024. Total assets grew to $127.7 million in March 31, 2025, from $113.1 million in June 30, 2024.

news.summary: In Madison, Wisconsin on May 15, 2025, a recent survey conducted by Nordic®, a healthcare consulting company, and Modern Healthcare, highlighted significant gaps in the preparedness of U.S. healthcare executives for implementing artificial intelligence (AI).
